sections.paragraph:
ἀναβιόω (Aristoph., Andoc., Pla. et al., also Artapanus: 726 Fgm. 3, 25 Jac. [in Eus., PE 9, 27, 25]) come to life again (Artem. 2, 62; 4, 82; Palaeph. p. 60, 6; Philostrat., Vi. Apoll. 1, 1, 1; 8, 7 p. 324, 27, Heroicus 1, 4; Jos., Ant. 18, 14) 2 Cl 19:4.—DELG s.v. βίος.

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Andoc Andoc , V–IVb.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Artapanus Artapanus , II b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Palaeph Palaeph , date uncertain—List 5

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 59
